column4: User Story
As a Provider, I should be able to receive a welcome email with a Set Password link when my profile is created.
As a Provider, on my first login I should be able to set password
As a Provider, after entering my credentials I should receive a One-Time Password (OTP) on my registered email
As a Provider, I should be able to resend the OTP if I did not receive it or if it has expired.
As a Provider, I should be able to login using valid credentials.
As a Provider, I should be able to reset the password if I forgot the password.
As a provider, I should be able to capture and view which source a lead originated from
As a provider, I should be able to assign service type (ADH, ALF, etc.) to a lead
As a provider, I should be able to assign leads to sales team members.
As a provider, I should be able to document lead notes and track follow-ups.
As a provider, I should be able to manage the sales pipeline.
As a provider, I should be able to track empty bed/room capacity.
As a provider, I should be able to track lead qualification and win/loss reasons.
As a provider, I should be able to analyze historical conversion data.
As a provider, I should be able to add lead details
As a provider, I should be able to view the list of leads
As a provider, I should be able to import & export list of leads.
As a provider, I should be able to add filter on lead list
As a provider, I should be able to edit lead details
As a provider, I should be able to view activity overview of Lead
As a Provider, I should be able Reject the lead with reason
As a Provider, I should be able to select call type, add pre-call notes, initiate a call with a lead and add in-call notes.
As a Provider, I should be able to see the automatically generated AI call summary for each call.
As a provider, I should be able to send SMS to lead
As a Provider, I should be able to see the automatically generated AI SMS summary .
As a provider, I should be able to add private notes to a lead
As a provider, I should be able to add public notes to a lead
As a Provider, I should be able to schedule a facility tour / Consultation visit with lead
As a Provider, I should be able to view scheduled visits in a calendar interface (Day, Week, and Month views), including both upcoming and past visits.
As a Provider, I should be able to view Scheduled visits in List view.
As a provider, I should be able to edit the scheduled visit with Lead from both calender and list view.
As a Provider, I should be able to apply filter on Scheduled visits
As a provider, I should be able to add Scheduled visit notes
As a provider, I should be able to view and send the the private pay rate card to Lead
As a provider, I should be able to view and send the fixed, state-mandated Medicaid rates for Medicaid patients as configured by the super admin,

column3: Select Case Manager and send documents
column4: As a Provider, I should be able to select a Case Manager and send the required documents for the lead
As a provider, I should be able to View and Send patient required and optional documents to the patient. e.g; Intake form, Contract  etc.
As a provider, I should be able to mark each mandatory document as received/not received
As a Provider, I should be able to resend the documents to the patient if required.
As a provider, I should be able to view and sign documents received from Patient
As a Provider, I should be able to send signature reminders to pending signatories.
As a provider, I should be able to view the Medicaid qualification probability score for a lead.

ADH, ALF : ( If Medicaid )
Resident - InProgress
column4: As a provider, I should be able to send state transport request to Verida with details
As a provider, I should be able to add the assigned case manager's details to the Verida transport request
As a provider, I should be able to add an escalation flag or escalation / Urgent details to the Verida transport request
As a Provider, I should be able to fill out state-provided details in the system, including CPT/ICD-10 codes, service frequencies, and validation of NPI numbers.
As a provider, I should be able to view the complete history of frequency changes
As a Provider, I should be able to track PA submission status (Submitted/Under Review/Approved/Denied).
As a provider, I should be able to resubmit updated document if got rejected from State
As a provider, I should be able to add Billing and clearance details for patient
As a provider, I should be able to convert a Lead to 'New Arrival' status after all documentation steps are completed and PA package is received.
As a provider, I should be able to convert a 'New Arrival' to 'Active' status after confirming arrival and completing service day setup.

As a provider, I should be able to view an activity timeline for a lead showing all activities with timestamps
- Lead Created
- Visit Done
- Phone Call
- SMS
- Visit Scheduled
As a Provider, I should be able to click on "View Summary" on activity to expand activity details
As a Provider, I should be able to check bed availability for both private and semi-private against state license capacity in real-time.
As a Provider, I should be able to view current census showing occupied beds, available beds, and beds on hold.
As a Provider, I should be able to filter a floor, choose a room type (private or semi-private), and assign a specific available room to a patient.
As a Provider, I should be able to move qualified resident to waitlist when no beds are available.
As a Provider, I should be able to view waitlist with resident priority, date added, and contact information.
As a Provider, I should be able to notify waitlisted resident when bed becomes available.
As a Provider, I should be able to track how long Leads have been on waitlist.
As a Provider, I should be able to update the room status on hold and release the room

As a Provider, I should be able to add a new patient by adding below info
Patient details ((address, phones, language)
Contact details
Emergency Contact details
Insurance Policy Information
Privacy consent
Photo capture
As a provider, I should be able to detect duplicate patient records based on name, DOB, and ID to prevent duplicate enrollments and maintain data integrity.

As a Provider, I should be able to view all residents in table/list format.
- All, Active, In-progress, New Arrivals and Discharged residents
As a Provider, I should be able to filter and Search residents list.
As a Provider, I should be able to import & export residents list.
As a Provider, I should be able to view resident basic details in header bar
As Provider, I should be able to update resident profiles, payers, contacts and preferences
As a Provider, I should be able to view and edit frequency schedule
As a Provider, I should be able to view transportation provider (VERIDA label), authorization expiration date, and usage progress bar (e.g., 30/90 days).
As a Provider, I should be able to view attendance card showing current month, attendance count (e.g., 06/31), and progress bar with attendance rate.
As staff, I should be able to log participant check-ins and check-outs for the day program
As caregivers, I should be able to record vitals for day program participants
As staff, I should be able to send families a summary of daily participation

As a staff member, I should be able to view daily rosters organized by program type (ADHS, ALF, Memory Care) and transportation route, showing expected members, arrival times, and any special care instructions for the day.

As a staff member, I should be able to check in members when they arrive at the center and check them out when they depart, with automatic timestamp recording and real-time attendance status updates visible to all authorized staff.
As a Provider, I should be able to view Trip pickup / drop-off assignments for each driver/family
As a Provider, I should be able to assign / reassign drivers to Trip  routes.
As a Provider, I should be able to view driver capacity and current trip assignments
As a Provider, I should be able to import trip attendance data from CSV file.
As a Provider, I should be able to mark resident as transported or no-show for Trips
As a Provider, I should be able to receive automated dashboard alert 60 days before Standing Order expiration.
As a Provider, I should be able to modify Standing Order for changes in service days or times.

As a staff member, I should be able to record full day, half-day or partial-day attendance by documenting actual arrival and departure times, with the system automatically calculating attendance duration and adjusting billable hours accordingly for members who attend only part of their scheduled day.

As a staff member, I should be able to mark members as absent and select the specific reason (hospitalization, scheduled vacation, illness, no-show, family emergency) with optional notes, enabling tracking of absence patterns and billing adjustments.
As a Provider, I should be able to complete Member Information Form (EDWP) with absence details.
As a Provider, I should be able to send email completed EDWP form to state case manager.
As a Provider, I should be able to view daily attendance roster with attendance type for all residents.
As a Provider, I should be able to track EDWP electronic sign-off status from state case manager.

As a Provider, I should be able to add resident participation in the activities :
 -Therapy activities
- Personal Care activities
- Participation in Meals
- Social Activities

As a Provider, I should be able to edit resident participation in the activities :
 -Therapy activities
- Personal Care activities
- Participation in Meals
- Social Activities
As a Provider, I should be able to copy activities from master template to all present residents for the day.
As a Provider, I should be able to view daily activity summary for each residents showing all recorded activities.
As a Provider, I should be able to generate and export Monthly Attendance Report for previous month's services.
As a provider, I should be able to plan and sequence transportation routes with pickup and drop-off stops optimized for efficiency.
As a provider, I should be able to track vehicle locations in real-time and capture GPS coordinates and timestamps for each pickup and drop-off for verification and billing.
As staff, I should be able to view all rooms and assign residents to available rooms
As a nurse, I should be able to create customized ALF service plans for each resident
As billing staff, I should be able to generate monthly rent and service billing for each resident

As a Provider, I should be able to select payment type
- Medicaid
- Private Pay
- Insurance
- Insurance + Medicaid
- Insurance + copay
- Medicaid + copay
As a Provider, I should be able to add Billing details for Medicaid patient e.g Prior-auth, frequency etc.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit Billing details for Medicaid patient e.g Prior-auth etc.
As a Provider, I should be able to add single and multiple payers.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit each payer's information
As a Provider, I should be able to select Card, Cash, Money order, Check payment option.
As a Provider, I should be able to add card details.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit card details.
As a Provider, I should be able to perform split (Equal Split or Custom Split).
As a Provider, I should be able to generate separate invoices for each payer based on split configuration.
As a Provider, I should be able to add  Insurace details in Insurance tab.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit Insurace details in Insurance tab.
As a Provider, I should be able to add billing details for Insurance + Medicaid payment.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit billing details for Insurance + Medicaid payment.
As a Provider, I should be able to set billing split for both insurance and Medicaid portions.
As a Provider, I should be able to set mixed billing with insurance as primary and Medicaid as secondary.
As a Provider, I should be able to add billing details for mixed Copay + Insurance.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit billing details for mixed Copay + Insurance.
As a Provider, I should be able to add billing details for mixed Medicaid + copay.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit billing details for mixed Medicaid + copay.
As a Provider, I should be able to select Card, Cash, Money order, Check payment option.
As a Provider, I should be able to add card details.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit  card details.
As a Provider, I should be able to perform split (Equal Split or Custom Split).
As a Provider, I should be able to select facility service model (A La Carte or Blended Rate).
As a Provider, I should be able to configure A La Carte model to track individual services (Room, Board, Laundry, Transport).
As a Provider, I should be able to configure Blended Rate model with single bundle price and no individual service tracking.
As a Provider, I should be able to view service model description and billing implications before selection.
As a Provider, I should be able to generate rate card showing pricing for Private room or Semi-private room.
As a Provider, I should be able to view rate card interface with all applicable charges and services.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit rate card
As a Provider, I should be able to apply discount to rate card if applicable.

column3: Send Rate Card and Welcome Email
column4: As a Provider, I should be able to send rate card and welcome email together to lead/family.
As a Provider, I should be able to collect deposit through multiple payment methods.
As a Provider, I should be able to record receipt of a security deposit, automatically update the resident status to "DEPOSIT RECEIVED", and send the deposit receipt to the responsible party.
As a Provider, I should be able to schedule RN assessment
As Provider, I should be able to add assessment details in the Assessment form.
As a Provider, I should be able to edit pre-admission assessment.
As a Provider, I should be able to send assessment to RN  for electronic signature
As a Provider, I should be able to view completed assessment to determine if facility can manage care level.
As a Provider, I should be able to approve / reject admission
As a Provider, I should be able to notify family/responsible party of decline decision
As a Provider, I should be able to release room hold and make room available again upon decline.
As a Provider, I should be able to assign specific room/suite to approved resident after resident admission approves.
As a Provider, I should be able to change room status from HOLD to OCCUPIED.
As a Provider, I should be able to add move-in date for resident.
As a Provider, I should be able to update resident status to ACTIVE upon admission approval.
As a Provider, I should be able to automatically update census report with Occupied Beds +1, Available Beds -1., Deposit Received -1.
As a Provider, I should be able to click on the “Discharge” action from the resident list.
As a Provider, I should be able to fill all the discharge details with mandatory discharge reason from predefined categories (Cannot Skip).
As a Provider, I should be able to add specific events leading to discharge decision.
As a Provider, I should be able to fill mandatory discharge survey/comment

As a Privider, I should be able to select and send notification to 
- Parent/Guardian
- Primary Caregiver
- Care team members
- Case Manager with EDWP  mail

As a Provider, I should be able to view the EDWP form once it has been signed and returned by the Case Manager, and the signed EDWP should be stored under a dedicated sub-tab within the Documents section.
As a Provider, I should be able to fill staff discharge survey  (Final Evaluation) form.
As a Provider, I should be able to add resident/family discharge survey capturing their feedback.
As a Provider, I should be able to add quality of care rating from resident/family.
As a Provider, I should be able to preview discharge confirmation details.
As a Provider, I should be able to create final medication list showing all medications at discharge.
As a Provider, I should be able to include any outstanding balances on final invoice.
As a Provider, I should be able to apply any applicable deductions (unpaid balances, damages) to security deposit.
As a Provider, I should be able to calculate final refund amount after all deductions.
As a Provider, I should be able to generate final billing invoice showing all services through discharge date.
As a Provider, I should be able to initiate a refund request that goes through a three-level approval workflow
As a Provider, I should be able to send refund check to responsible party
As a Provider, I should be able to release discharged resident's room and mark as AVAILABLE.
As a Provider, I should be able to automatically update census report with Occupied Beds -1, Available Beds +1.
As a Provider, I should be able to archive complete resident record with 7-year retention for compliance.
As a Provider, I should be able to preserve all historical data including clinical, financial, and administrative records.
As a provider, I should be able to create new visit notes from patients' charts.
As a provider, I should be able to add, update, or delete flags for patients.
As a provider, I should be able to add note for other staff member.
As a provider, I should be able to send message to patients or providers.

As a scheduler, I should receive immediate alerts when attempting to assign staff to overlapping shifts.
As a scheduler, I should be able to view each staff member's available, unavailable, and preferred time slots.
As a scheduler, I should be able to create open shifts by specifying date, time, duration, location, program type, and required skills.
As a scheduler, I should be able to create NEMT routes by specifying pickup/drop-off locations, times, and patient details.
As a Provider, I should be able to view the monthly, weekly and daily schedule of visits / appointments
As a Provider, I should be able to create a new appointment for a member directly from the calendar
As a Provider, I should be able to search for a member by name to find their appointments.
As a Provider, I should be able to filter the visits appointments.

As a compliance manager, I should be able to view a dashboard showing all compliance tasks categorized by status (completed, upcoming within 7 days, overdue), filterable by facility, task type, and date range, with visual alerts for critical overdue items and one-click access to complete or review any task

As a compliance manager, I should be able to create recurring compliance tasks with flexible schedules (daily, weekly, monthly, quarterly, annually) that automatically generate on the specified intervals, assign to responsible staff, and send reminders before due dates to ensure timely completion.

As a staff member, I should be able to digitally record compliance activities including fire drills, refrigerator temperature checks, generator tests, emergency equipment inspections, and facility safety walkthroughs with date/time stamps, staff signatures, and recorded values or observations.

As a staff member, I should be able to attach supporting evidence to compliance logs including photos (equipment readings, facility conditions, completed repairs), scanned documents (inspection reports, certificates, vendor invoices), and notes to create a complete audit trail for regulatory reviews.
As a user, I should be able to see all contacts linked to the resident in one place.

 1. personal
 2.Guardian
 3 POA
 4. Representative payee
 5. Emergency-only
As a user, I should be able to update & remove an existing contact’s information.

As a Nurse Manager, I want the system to require confirmation that all associated corrective actions are complete and notifications are tracked before an incident can be marked as closed, so that no step in the resolution process is overlooked.

As a provider, I should be able to generate draft progress notes with one click by leveraging structured data including vitals, ADLs, and patient history.
As a provider, I should receive AI suggestions for documentation phrasing that aligns with regulatory standards and best practices.
As a provider, I should be alerted when required elements are missing from clinical documentation before submission.
As a provider, I should be alerted when there are sudden changes in a member's attendance patterns that may indicate health or social issues.
As a provider, I should receive automatic flags when a member exhibits patterns of frequent falls or emergency room visits.
As a provider, I should be able to view AI-generated risk scores predicting likelihood of hospitalization or institutionalization.

As a provider, I should be able to run claims through an AI-based scrubber before submission that validates CPT/ICD-10 code pairings, checks for missing required fields, identifies inconsistencies against PA records, assigns a denial risk score based on historical rejection patterns, and flags high-risk claims for manual review with suggested corrective actions
As a provider, I should receive AI suggestions for appropriate modifiers and diagnostic codes based on encounter documentation details.
As a provider, I should receive AI-powered staffing recommendations based on census projections, patient acuity levels, and required skill mix.
As a provider, I should receive AI suggestions for optimal transportation routes considering traffic patterns, member locations, and time windows.
As a provider, I should receive automated reminders for expiring credentials, authorizations, and compliance tasks with configurable advance notice.

As a provider, I want calls made through the RingCentral integration to be automatically transcribed in real-time, with the transcript attached to the lead's or resident's communication timeline, so that no verbal information is lost and all call content is searchable and reviewable.

As a provider, I want AI to generate a structured call summary after each inquiry or follow-up call, extracting key information including: patient condition discussed, medical history highlights, family concerns raised, decision outcome, and recommended next action items

As a provider, I should receive AI-generated, patient-specific clinical recommendations (e.g., medication review needed, fall risk protocol activation, therapy adjustment, hydration monitoring) based on the individual resident's health history, recent vitals, incident patterns, and care activity trends, so that I can make proactive care decisions rather than only reacting to events.

As a provider, after recording a pain scale entry for a resident, AI should automatically compare the score against the resident's established baseline and flag any sudden or significant increase, alerting the assigned RN and facility director so that pain escalation is caught early and intervention can be initiated.

As a Nurse/RN, I want the AI to analyze a new resident's captured medical conditions and ICD-10 codes so that it can suggest the most relevant care plan templates from the master library of 30-50 templates, reducing manual search time and ensuring the most appropriate templates are selected at onboarding.

As a Provider, I want the system to suggest specific safety interventions (e.g., fall protocols or diabetic monitoring) based on flagged risk scores and vital sign baselines captured during the initial assessment.

As a Provider, I want the AI to pre-populate the "Problem" and "Approach" fields in a suggested template using the resident's specific clinical findings so that I only need to review and set the personalized goals.

As a Provider, I want the AI to verify that the selected care plan templates and suggested documentation phrasing align with state-specific regulatory standards for the facility's location (e.g., Georgia vs. Washington vs. Texas), so that all clinical records are compliant with the applicable state's requirements without manual cross-referencing of state regulations.

column3: AI Scribe for Incident Intervention Guidance
column4: As a Provider, when I am documenting a major incident, the AI Scribe should prompt me through a guided intervention checklist based on the incident type (e.g., for a fall: "What are the symptoms? Has the hospital been called? Is the patient showing signs of concussion? Has the family been notified?" — for a wandering incident: "Where was the resident found? How long were they missing? Were doors/alarms functioning?"), so that I follow correct clinical protocol and capture all required information even when a supervisor or RN is not available on-site at 2 AM.

As a provider, the system should automatically generate draft monthly progress notes for every active resident at the start of each monthly documentation cycle, pulling data from the past 30 days of vitals, ADLs, medication changes, incidents, and care plan updates, so that clinical staff can review and finalize rather than write from scratch each month.

As a provider, when a lead is captured from A Place for Mom, Caring.com, or other referral sources, AI should automatically suggest a qualification status by analyzing the lead's profile data (care needs, medical conditions, age, location, payment type) against the facility's current bed availability, service types offered, and staffing capacity, so that the marketing/sales team can prioritize the highest-probability leads for immediate follow-up.

As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to search for documents by name, file type, date, or organization across the entire system.
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to filter documents by date uploaded, file type, organization, facility, or document category.
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to sort documents by name, date modified, file size, or uploaded by.
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to navigate through the folder structure using breadcrumb navigation so that I always know my current location in the directory.
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to view a list of recently uploaded or accessed documents for quick reference.
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to view the complete version history of a document showing who uploaded each version and when.
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to restore a previous version of a document when the current version needs to be rolled back.
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to compare two versions of the same document to identify what changed between versions.
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to share a document with specific organizations, facilities, or individual users directly from the system.

As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to view total storage usage across all organizations and facilities so that I can monitor and manage system storage capacity.
As a SuperAdmin, deleted documents should be moved to a trash folder and retained for a defined period before permanent deletion so that accidental deletions can be recovered.
As a SuperAdmin, I should be able to restore a document from the trash folder before it is permanently deleted.

As a provider, I should be able to electronically enter and maintain a complete medication order profile (including drug name, dose, route, and schedule) for each resident, so that the MAR is always accurate and reflects the current physician's orders.
As a provider, I should be able to view a resident's complete MAR for the current day, so that I can quickly see all medications due for that specific person.

As a provider, I should be able to view a consolidated MAR list of all medications due for my entire assigned group/floor during the current shift/time block, so that I can efficiently plan and execute my medication rounds.

As a provider, I should be able to document the outcome of a medication administration (Given, Refused, Held, Not available, Late), so that the resident's health record is accurate and any necessary follow-up is prompted.

As a provider, I should be able to the system to require a second nurse's login and electronic signature before documenting the administration of High-Risk/Insulin/Controlled medications, so that safety is maximized and errors are prevented.

As a provider, I should be able to be prompted to document both the reason for administering a PRN (as-needed) medication AND the resident's response/effect one hour later, so that I can assess the medication's effectiveness and ensure proper care.

As a provider, I should be able to the system to alert me immediately if a scheduled medication is 15 minutes past its due time without an outcome documented, so that I can intervene quickly and ensure patient safety.
As a provider, when documenting a late medication, I want the system to prompt me to document the specific reason for the delay, so that there is an audit trail for all deviations from the schedule.

As an Approved Family Contact, I need a real-time feed/timeline showing non-critical updates (e.g., activity attendance, minor staff notes, status changes) with a configurable display filter, so that I can feel connected to my loved one's day-to-day life.

As an Administrator or Staff Member, I need to be able to configure detailed privacy settings for staff notes, specifying which types of notes are visible to the family portal and which are internal-only, so that we maintain clinical confidentiality while sharing relevant information.

As an Approved Family Contact, I need to receive immediate, high-priority notifications (via Push, Email, and/or SMS) for critical events like falls, hospitalizations, or significant medication changes, so that I can respond quickly and be fully informed in emergencies.

As an Administrator, I need to be able to send outbound broadcast messages (e.g., weather closures, holiday schedules) to all or selected family contacts via the portal, email, and/or SMS, so that I can communicate important organizational updates efficiently.

As a billing coordinator, the system should automatically generate charges from ADHS attendance, Home Care EVV visits, ALF room & board/services, NEMT trips, and Behavioral Health encounters, applying correct CPT codes, rates, and unit calculations based on service type. All charges should link to valid authorizations and prevent billing beyond authorized limits or outside date ranges.

As a billing coordinator, I should be able to manually enter charges for special services, supplies, assessments, or adjustments by selecting patient, service date, charge code, units, and rate. The system should validate against active authorizations, prevent duplicates, and require supporting documentation for audit purposes.

As a billing supervisor, I should be able to review pending charges in a work queue organized by status (ready, missing auth, rate issues, duplicates, holds), filter by payer/date/type, and view source documentation. I should be able to approve, reject with comments, or hold charges, and batch-process approved charges for claims generation
As a provider, I should be able to import ERA files and automatically post payments to corresponding claims and service lines without manual entry.
As a provider, I should be able to track patient responsibility amounts including copays and deductibles that transfer to patient accounts for collection.
As a provider, I should be able to configure automatic write-off rules for contractual adjustments and small balance amounts based on payer contracts.
As a provider, I should be able to reconcile payment batches by payer and bank deposit to ensure all payments are properly posted.
As a provider I should be able to start encounter and enter progress note
As a user, I should be able to select an encounter type (ADHS daily, ALF shift, BH session, care coordination) so the note follows the correct structured format.
As a user, I should be able to complete predefined fields for each encounter type so documentation remains consistent and compliant.
As a user, I should be able to document notes in SOAP format so I can follow a clinical standard for problem-focused charting.
As a user, I should be able to document notes in a narrative format so I can capture unstructured or descriptive information when needed.
As a user, I should see program, staff, location, and last vitals automatically filled into the note so I don’t have to re-enter repeated information.
As a user, I should be able to edit auto-populated context fields so that any incorrect information can be corrected before saving the note.
As a user, I should be able to attach files, photos, or wound images to an encounter so supporting information is stored with the clinical note.
As a user, I should be able to preview or download attached files so I can review documentation anytime from the patient chart.

As a Provider, I should be able to change the encounter note format by selecting a note template from a dropdown menu, so that I can easily adapt my documentation to different clinical scenarios and maintain consistency in my notes.
As a Provider, I should be able to view &  import previous encounter notes into current encounter.

As a Provider, I should be able to quickly access a library of customizable templates within my note-writing interface and seamlessly import relevant template data into my current note so I can save time on documentation, maintain consistency, and ensure accuracy in my patient records.
As a Provider, I should be able to view or edit encounter summary.
As a Provider, I should be able to Print encounter summary.
As a Provider, I should be able to sign and lock encounter.
As a provider, I should be able to view a list of Unsigned Encounters and able to sign encounters.

column3: Edit and sign off Unsigned Encounter
column4: As a provider, I should be able to view and edit and sign off Unsigned Encounter.
As a provider, I should be able to filter Unsigned Encounter by status or name.

As a Caregiver, I want to be able to quickly document the completion and specifics of core ADLs (e.g., bathing, dressing, toileting) immediately after performing them, so that the resident's care record is current and accurate.

As a Caregiver, I want to be able to select the exact level of assistance provided for each ADL (e.g., Independent, Minimal Assist, Total Assist), so that the resident's needs are accurately reflected and future staffing is planned correctly.

As a Nurse/Manager, I want to be able to configure standard daily and weekly task templates based on the resident's specific program or care level (e.g., Memory Care vs. Assisted Living), so that care plans can be deployed quickly and consistently across similar residents.
As a Nurse, I want to be able to customize the generated daily tasks for an individual resident based on their unique needs and physician orders, so that the care plan is truly person-centered.

As a Caregiver, I want to view a clear, prioritized checklist of all assigned care tasks (ADLs and other duties) for my current shift/time block, so that I can efficiently execute my rounds and ensure nothing is missed.
As a Nurse Manager, I want to be able to view the real-time status of task completion for all caregivers on the floor, so that I can intervene promptly if tasks are falling behind schedule.

As a Family Member, I want to be able to view a printable, easy-to-read summary of the ADLs and care tasks completed for my loved one over the past day or week, so that I can stay informed about their daily care.
As a user, I should be able to view the list of tasks and their details according to the resident’s admission details
As a user, I should be able to view the residents’ completion percentage & count of completed documents
As a user, I should be able to see the status of a task (Get started, Upload, Shared, Waiting for other signature)
As a user, I should be able to remove a task if it is obsolete or duplicated so that the task list is accurate
As a user, I should be able to send & receive email reminders for pending tasks so that deadlines are not missed
As a user, I should be able to share tasks with the responsible person for signature or verification
As a User, I should be able to download document
As a User, I should be able to fill & sign document

column3: Upload Supporting Files to fill assessment
column4: As a user, I should be able to upload supporting files to fill assesment for a task so that all relevant documents are attached
As a user, I should be able to view completed task documents under the Documents module for reference

As an authorized user (Executive Director, RCC, or Nurse), I should be able to complete a comprehensive resident assessment, with system automatically transferring data to create care plan template, pre-populating sections where resident needs assistance (minimal/moderate/maximum) and excluding items where resident is independent, showing which items were auto-populated vs manually added

As an authorized user, when I update a resident's assessment (e.g., assistance level changes), the system should prompt me to update care plan and automatically update future daily checklists while preserving historical records, with family notification for significant changes

 - Inbox
 - Sent
 - Archive
As a provider, I should be able to create new messages to all practice member by adding subject and body content.
As a provider, I should be able to send message to other provider and able to adding associated patients.
As a provider, I should be able to view message details.
As a provider, I should be able to delete messages and able to archive that messages.

As a Clinician or Admin, I need to select a pre-defined Service Plan Template based on the patient's enrolled Program (e.g., Home Care Template) when creating a new care plan, so that I have a standardized starting point containing common ADLs, goals, and interventions for that service type.

As a Clinician, I need to add, modify, and delete specific patient Goals (e.g., "Improve mobility"), Interventions (e.g., "Assist with ambulation 3x daily"), and Activities of Daily Living (ADLs) to customize the service plan, so that it accurately reflects the patient's individual needs.

As a Clinician, I need to assign a Review Date to each Goal and Intervention, and have a tracking mechanism (e.g., status updates) for the completion/progress of interventions, so that I can monitor effectiveness and schedule necessary re-assessments.
As a user, I should be able to select areas of need for a resident and set objectives, frequency, responsible person, plan, and evaluation method.
As a system, it should be able to generate a structured service plan based on the user’s input.
As a system, it should be able to display assigned points for each category to show overall care requirements.
As a user, I should be able to collect signatures from responsible members to confirm agreement with the plan.
As a provider, I should be able to maintain an inventory catalog for PPE, food, medical supplies, and equipment used across the facility.
As a provider, I should be able to track inventory levels by storage location within the facility to manage stock efficiently.
As a provider, I should be able to record inventory transactions for receiving shipments, using supplies, and making adjustments to maintain accurate stock counts.
As a provider, I should be able to receive automatic alerts when inventory falls below minimum levels with suggestions for reorder quantities.
